channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: No, this is not a new Trashcan Mac Pro: Mac Mini-inspired desktop hybrid NAS sees daylight
item.description: ORICO MiniTower, with its Mac Mini-inspired design, offers hybrid storage of two 24TB HDDs and an 8TB SSD.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: 5 GenAI principles for K-12 education
item.description: Imagine this: A new technology has arrived, drawing enormous public discussion. Among the questions is how it might be used in schools. Advocates call for its widespread adoption, saying it will revolutionize schooling and motivate students. Others are wary, fearing it will make kids lazy.
item.dc:creator: Jack Lynch, Fast Company

item.title: For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness
item.description: Current and former executives say the federal technology themes for 2025 will be similar to 2024 with the addition of efficiency and effectiveness. The post For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oracle refuses to yield JavaScript trademark, Deno Land says
item.description: JavaScript runtime provider Deno Land’s efforts to get Oracle to yield the trademark for JavaScript have hit a snag, with Oracle refusing to voluntarily withdraw the trademark, Deno Land said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Red Bull and Ford are building a new F1 hybrid race car engine—first as bits, then atoms
item.description: To get from 0 to 60 in Formula 1 engine design while competing against organizations with much more experience, Red Bull Ford Powertrains will need extra help (and, no, that boost won’t come in the form of a certain energy drink).
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: Happy New Year! We begin the new year with the top five procurement themes that will shape the federal acquisition marketplace in 2025 and beyond
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: AI will transform the enterprise ‘in a generation,’ say CDOs
item.description: Despite the many concerns around generative AI, businesses are continuing to explore the technology and put it into production, the 2025 AI and Data Leadership Executive Benchmark Survey revealed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The biggest addiction treatment provider in the US says it was hit by data breach
item.description: BayMark Health Services notified law enforcement and affected people of the breach, offering free identity theft monitoring.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: More than 4% PC shipment growth predicted for 2025, but not for what you expect, says IDC
item.description: PC sales certainly weren’t going gangbusters in 2024: They only grew a paltry 1% over 2023. According to new figures from IDC, vendors shipped 262.7 million PCs in 2024. But things did pick up a bit in Q4 2024: Shipments grew 1.8% over the prior year, reaching 68.9 million.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg Met with President-elect Trump Before Announcing Fact-checking Program Elimination
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin reveals that Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg met with President-elect Trump a day before announcing the elimination of its fact-checking program to prioritize free speech.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Now, you can create a digital copy of your personality in just two hours
item.description: Researchers at Google Deepmind and Stanford University have concluded that a two-hour interview is sufficient to create a realistic AI copy with the same personality as the interviewee.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk-Trump relationship not a 'concern': Hyundai CEO
item.description: Hyundai CEO José Muñoz said he’s not concerned about the relationship between President-elect Trump and Tesla CEO Elon Musk, whose company is one of Hyundai’s biggest competitors. Instead, Munoz said in an interview with Bloomberg that he thinks their close relationship could benefit the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Uncertainty over Trump’s electric vehicle policies clouds 2025 forecast for carmakers
item.description: S&P Global Mobility expects global sales of 15.1 million battery electric vehicles in 2025, which would mark a 30% jump. Battery electric vehicles are expected to make up 16.7% of the market share for light vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: US warns of risk from most of world’s online pharmacies
item.description: The Office of the U.S. Trade Representative warned there’s a risk of ineffective or dangerous drugs from online pharmacies that are being illegally run. In a report released Wednesday, the office singled out 19 countries that raised concerns of possible movement of counterfeit or pirated drugs.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Apple doubles down on privacy after Siri-snooping settlement
item.description: Apple has vehemently denied that it ever abused recordings of Siri requests by using those records for marketing, ad sales, or any of the other creepy nonsense we’re being forced to tolerate with other connected devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the globe, at least 4 in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: CompTIA bolsters penetration testing certification
item.description: CompTIA recently upgraded its PenTest+ certification program to educate professionals on cybersecurity penetration testing with training for artificial intelligence (AI), scanning and analysis, and vulnerability management, among other things.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok's fate arrives at Supreme Court in collision of free speech and national security
item.description: In a collision between free speech and national security, the Supreme Court will hear arguments over the fate of TikTok, a digital age cultural phenomenon that roughly half the U.S. population uses for entertainment and information.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How AI will shape work in 2025
item.description: AlphaSense is a market intelligence platform that uses generative artificial intelligence (genAI) and natural language processing to help organizations find and analyze insights from sources like financial reports, news, earnings calls, and proprietary documents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google faces privacy lawsuit as judge highlights data governance concerns
item.description: A federal judge in San Francisco has ruled that a privacy lawsuit against Google, alleging the company improperly collected personal data from mobile devices, can proceed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS to invest $11 billion in Georgia to expand infra for gen AI
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is planning to invest $11 billion in the state of Georgia to expand its infrastructure in the US to support the growing demand for generative AI, AI, and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025
item.description: AI in action: How agencies are building out trusted artificial intelligence capabilities The post Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Sam Altman’s sister accuses OpenAI CEO of sexually abusing her
item.description: She accused the OpenAI chief executive officer of sexually abusing her for almost a decade, in a lawsuit filed in federal court.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Intro to Ktor: The HTTP server for Kotlin
item.description: Kotlin is a modern general-purpose language that brings together object-oriented and functional programming power in a sleek syntax. Ktor is the official HTTP server for Kotlin, leveraging Kotlin’s expressiveness for building endpoints and other server-side necessities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security
item.description: The global IT skills shortage is predicted to impact 90% of organizations by 2026, according to IDC. The post Federal IT skills shortage: How AI can help relieve the gap and improve security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Why Apple’s AI-driven reality distortion matters
item.description: Apple has been forced to admit what every company involved in artificial intelligence (AI) should also be forced to state — AI makes mistakes, just like people do. On the surface, it’s not a terribly big deal: Apple’s AI badly mangled a handful of news headlines.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Malicious apps posing as VPNs can turn your device into a tool for cyberattacks
item.description: Not all apps out there offer legit virtual private networks, according to Kurt the CyberGuy. He explains how to keep your information safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Create custom visuals on your iPhone with Image Playground in iOS 18.2
item.description: Apple's Image Playground transforms text prompts into images or animations in seconds, brining a world of creativity to the palm of your hand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's Martian helicopter promises unprecedented views of the red planet
item.description: NASA's Ingenuity helicopter, which flew on Mars in April 2021, became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on a planet other than Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
